title: Slow-Cook Shattering Australian Coriander Bake
description: A winey, shattering, hangover-cure Australian dish featuring coriander, perfect for lazy mornings. The coriander and spices will tantalize your taste buds and leave you wanting more.
instructions: Rub the pork shoulder with salt, black pepper, cumin, and coriander. Place the pork shoulder in a slow cooker or Dutch oven.
In a blender or food processor, combine garlic, ginger, red wine, and beef broth. Blend until smooth and pour over the pork shoulder.
Cover the slow cooker or Dutch oven with a lid and cook on low for 8 hours or overnight.
Transfer the pork shoulder to a cutting board and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing it thinly.
In a large skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the sliced pork and cook until browned and crispy, about 5 minutes per side.
Serve the pork with the juices spooned over the top and garnished with chopped coriander.
If desired, serve with a side of roasted vegetables or a salad.
Garnish with additional chopped coriander, if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
